summary: twilio-sdk typosquats twilio and exfiltrates host identifiers via postinstall HTTP beacon
details: Package name twilio-sdk impersonates the official twilio npm SDK but ships an empty module (index.js: `module.exports = {};`). Its package.json declares a postinstall hook that runs an inline `node -e` script reading os.hostname(), process.env.CI, and process.version, then issues a plain HTTP GET to the hardcoded bare IPv4 endpoint http://46.224.67.169:3000/ping with those values as query parameters. This fires automatically on `npm install` with no opt-in. The combination — typosquat name targeting installs that mistype `twilio`, empty advertised functionality, auto-fired install-time beacon to a bare-IP HTTP endpoint sending host identifiers (hostname leaks corporate identity, CI flag distinguishes developer laptops from build infrastructure) — matches the supply-chain reconnaissance pattern. The README's 'security research honeypot' framing does not change the installer-side impact: any developer or build system that installs this package leaks host data to an attacker-controlled IP.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
